title: Hastinapur Sanctuary
tagline: Hastinapur Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Hastinapur Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in the Gangetic plains of Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Hastinapur Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in the Gangetic plains of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was established in 1986 and covers 2,073 km2 (800 sq mi) in Meerut, Muzzafarnagar, Ghaziabad, Bijnor, Hapur and Amroha districts.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Hastinapur
topPlaces.description: Hastinapur (Hastināpur) is a city in the Meerut district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Hastinapura is described as the capital of the Kuru kingdom in Hindu texts such as the Mahabharata and the Puranas; it is also mentioned in ancient Jain texts.

topPlaces.name: Jambudweep
topPlaces.description: Jambudweep is a Digambara Jain temple in Hastinapur, Uttar Pradesh built by the Jain nun Gyanmati in 1972. Jambudweep is a depiction of Jain cosmology Jambudvipa.

topPlaces.name: Digamber Jain Bada Mandir Hastinapur
topPlaces.description: Digamber Jain Bada Mandir Hastinapur is a Jain temple complex located in Hastinapur, Uttar Pradesh. It is the oldest Jain temple in Hastinapur dedicated to Shantinatha, the 16th Jain Tirthankara.

items.note: Hastinapur (Hastināpur) is a city in the Meerut district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Hastinapura is described as the capital of the Kuru kingdom in Hindu texts such as the Mahabharata and the Puranas; it is also mentioned in ancient Jain texts.
Jambudweep is a Digambara Jain temple in Hastinapur, Uttar Pradesh built by the Jain nun Gyanmati in 1972. Jambudweep is a depiction of Jain cosmology Jambudvipa.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Digamber Jain Bada Mandir Hastinapur

items.note: Digamber Jain Bada Mandir Hastinapur is a Jain temple complex located in Hastinapur, Uttar Pradesh. It is the oldest Jain temple in Hastinapur dedicated to Shantinatha, the 16th Jain Tirthankara.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hastinapur Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hastinapur Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hastinapur Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (114 km) and the nearest railway station is Moradabad Junction (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hastinapur Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hastinapur Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hastinapur Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Hastinapur Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hastinapur Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hastinapur, Jambudweep, Digamber Jain Bada Mandir Hastinapur.
